L. 102–240, § 3006(h)(1)105 Stat. 20905312(c)(3)49 App.:1607b (2d–last sentences).In subsections (a) and (b)(1), the words “(or the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development when required by )” are added for clarity.section 5334(i) of this titleIn subsection (a), the word “working” is omitted as surplus. The words “departments, agencies, and instrumentalities of the United States Government” are substituted for “other Federal departments and agencies” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code. The words “all phases of”, “(including the development, testing, and demonstration of new facilities, equipment, techniques, and methods)”, “In carrying out the provisions of this section”, “or data as he deems”, “public or private”, and “contained . . . or . . . other provision of” are omitted as surplus.section 1701d–3 of title 12In subsection (b)(1), before clause (A), the words “public and private”, “assist in establishing or carrying on comprehensive research in the problems of transportation in urban areas. Such grants shall be used to”, and “and qualified” are omitted as surplus. In clause (A), the words “or both” are omitted as surplus.In subsection (b)(3), the word “appropriate” is added for clarity.In subsection (c)(1), the words “and agencies thereof” are omitted as surplus.In subsection (c)(3), before clause (A), the words “public or private training” and “the sum of” are omitted as surplus. In clause (B), the words “in connection with the fellowship” are omitted as surplus.

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary shall provide assistance for projects and activities to advance innovative public transportation research and development in accordance with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Research, Development, Demonstration, and Deployment Projects.—
children.label: b Research, Development, Demonstration, and Deployment Projects.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may make grants and enter into contracts, cooperative agreements, and other agreements for research, development, demonstration, and deployment projects, and evaluation of research and technology of national significance to public transportation, that the Secretary determines will improve public transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: departments, agencies, and instrumentalities of the Government, including Federal laboratories;

children.label: B
children.text: State and local governmental entities;

children.label: C
children.text: providers of public transportation;

children.label: D
children.text: private or non-profit organizations;

children.label: E
children.text: institutions of higher education; and

children.label: F
children.text: technical and community colleges.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: To receive a grant, contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement under this section, an entity described in paragraph (2) shall submit an application to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: a statement of purpose detailing the need being addressed;

children.label: ii
children.text: the short- and long-term goals of the project, including opportunities for future innovation and development, the potential for deployment, and benefits to riders and public transportation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the short- and long-term funding requirements to complete the project and any future objectives of the project.

children.heading: Accelerated implementation and deployment of advanced digital construction management systems.—
children.label: 4 Accelerated implementation and deployment of advanced digital construction management systems.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and implement a program under this subsection to promote, implement, deploy, demonstrate, showcase, support, and document the application of advanced digital construction management systems, practices, performance, and benefits.

children.label: I
children.text: maximize interoperability with other systems, products, tools, or applications;

children.label: II
children.text: boost productivity;

children.label: III
children.text: manage complexity;

children.label: IV
children.text: reduce project delays and cost overruns;

children.label: V
children.text: enhance safety and quality; and

children.label: VI
children.text: reduce total costs for the entire lifecycle of transportation infrastructure assets;

children.label: ii
children.text: more timely and productive information-sharing among stakeholders through reduced reliance on paper to manage construction processes and deliverables such as blueprints, design drawings, procurement and supply-chain orders, equipment logs, daily progress reports, and punch lists;

children.label: iii
children.text: deployment of digital management systems that enable and leverage the use of digital technologies on construction sites by contractors, such as state-of-the-art automated and connected machinery and optimized routing software that allows construction workers to perform tasks faster, safer, more accurately, and with minimal supervision;

children.label: iv
children.text: the development and deployment of best practices for use in digital construction management;

children.label: I
children.text: to integrate the adoption of digital management systems and technologies in contracts; and

children.label: II
children.text: to weigh the cost of digitization and technology in setting project budgets;

children.label: I
children.text: to better manage projects using advanced construction management technologies; and

children.label: II
children.text: to properly measure and reward technology adoption across projects;

children.label: I
children.text: to report data relating to the project in digital formats; and

children.label: II
children.text: to fully capture the efficiencies and benefits of advanced digital construction management systems and related technologies;

children.label: viii
children.text: reduction in the environmental footprint of construction projects using advanced digital construction management systems resulting from elimination of congestion through more efficient projects; and

children.label: ix
children.text: enhanced worker and pedestrian safety resulting from increased transparency.

children.heading: .—Publication
children.label: C .—Publication
children.text: The reporting requirements for the accelerated implementation and deployment of advanced digital construction management systems program established under shall include data and analysis collected under this section.section 503(c)(5) of title 23

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a grant to or enter into a contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement under this section with an entity described in subsection (b)(2) to carry out a public transportation research project that has as its ultimate goal the development and deployment of new and innovative ideas, practices, and approaches.

children.label: i
children.text: seniors;

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: low-income individuals;

children.label: B
children.text: mobility management and improvements and travel management systems;

children.label: C
children.text: data and communication system advancements;

children.label: i
children.text: train control;

children.label: ii
children.text: capacity improvements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: performance management;

children.label: E
children.text: capital and operating efficiencies;

children.label: F
children.text: planning and forecasting modeling and simulation;

children.label: G
children.text: advanced vehicle design;

children.label: H
children.text: advancements in vehicle technology;

children.label: I
children.text: asset maintenance and repair systems advancement;

children.label: J
children.text: construction and project management;

children.label: K
children.text: alternative fuels;

children.label: L
children.text: the environment and energy efficiency;

children.label: M
children.text: safety improvements; or

children.label: N
children.text: any other area that the Secretary determines is important to advance the interests of public transportation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a grant to or enter into a contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement under this section with an entity described in subsection (b)(2) to carry out a public transportation innovation and development project that seeks to improve public transportation systems nationwide in order to provide more efficient and effective delivery of public transportation services, including through technology and technological capacity improvements.

children.label: A
children.text: the development of public transportation research projects that received assistance under subsection (c) that the Secretary determines were successful;

children.label: B
children.text: planning and forecasting modeling and simulation;

children.label: C
children.text: capital and operating efficiencies;

children.label: D
children.text: advanced vehicle design;

children.label: E
children.text: advancements in vehicle technology;

children.label: F
children.text: the environment and energy efficiency;

children.label: G
children.text: system capacity, including train control and capacity improvements; or

children.label: H
children.text: any other area that the Secretary determines is important to advance the interests of public transportation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may, under terms and conditions that the Secretary prescribes, make a grant to or enter into a contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement with an entity described in paragraph (2) to promote the early deployment and demonstration of innovation in public transportation that has broad applicability.

children.label: A
children.text: an entity described in subsection (b)(2); or

children.label: B
children.text: a consortium of entities described in subsection (b)(2), including a provider of public transportation, that will share the costs, risks, and rewards of early deployment and demonstration of innovation.

children.label: A
children.text: the deployment of research and technology development resulting from private efforts or Federally funded efforts;

children.label: B
children.text: the implementation of research and technology development to advance the interests of public transportation; or

children.label: C
children.text: the deployment of low or no emission vehicles, zero emission vehicles, or associated advanced technology.

children.heading: .—Evaluation
children.label: 4 .—Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which a project receives assistance under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall conduct a comprehensive evaluation of the success or failure of the projects funded under this subsection and any plan for broad-based implementation of the innovation promoted by successful projects.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: 5 .—Prohibition
children.text: The Secretary may not make grants under this subsection for the demonstration, deployment, or evaluation of a vehicle that is in revenue service unless the Secretary determines that the project makes significant technological advancements in the vehicle.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “direct carbon emissions” means the quantity of direct greenhouse gas emissions from a vehicle, as determined by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency;

children.label: i
children.text: a passenger vehicle used to provide public transportation that the Secretary determines sufficiently reduces energy consumption or harmful emissions, including direct carbon emissions, when compared to a comparable standard vehicle; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a zero emission vehicle used to provide public transportation; and

children.label: C
children.text: the term “zero emission vehicle” means a low or no emission vehicle that produces no carbon or particulate matter.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of each project that received assistance under this section during the preceding fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of each project described in paragraph (1), including any evaluation conducted under subsection (e)(4) for the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: C
children.text: a strategic research roadmap proposal for allocations of amounts for assistance under this section for the current and subsequent fiscal year, including anticipated work areas, proposed demonstrations and strategic partnership opportunities;

children.heading: .—Updates
children.label: 2 .—Updates
children.text: Not less than every 3 months, the Secretary shall update on the Web site of the Department of Transportation the information described in paragraph (1)(C) to reflect any changes to the Secretary’s plans to make assistance available under this section.

children.heading: .—Long-term research plans
children.label: 3 .—Long-term research plans
children.text: The Secretary is encouraged to develop long-term research plans and shall identify in the annual report under paragraph (1) and in updates under paragraph (2) allocations of amounts for assistance and notices of funding opportunities to execute long-term strategic research roadmap plans.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Government share of the cost of a project carried out under this section shall not exceed 80 percent, except that if there is substantial public interest or benefit, the Secretary may approve a greater Federal share.

children.heading: .—Non-government share
children.label: 2 .—Non-government share
children.text: The non-Government share of the cost of a project carried out under this section may be derived from in-kind contributions.

children.heading: .—Financial benefit
children.label: 3 .—Financial benefit
children.text: If the Secretary determines that there would be a clear and direct financial benefit to an entity under a grant, contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement under this section, the Secretary shall establish a Government share of the costs of the project to be carried out under the grant, contract, cooperative agreement, or other agreement that is consistent with the benefit.

children.heading: Low or No Emission Vehicle Component Assessment.—
children.label: h Low or No Emission Vehicle Component Assessment.—
A
children.text: the term “covered institution of higher education” means an institution of higher education with which the Secretary enters into a contract or cooperative agreement, or to which the Secretary makes a grant, under paragraph (2)(B) to operate a facility selected under paragraph (2)(A);

children.label: B
children.text: the terms “direct carbon emissions” and “low or no emission vehicle” have the meanings given those terms in subsection (e)(6);

children.label: C
children.text: the term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in section 102 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (); and20 U.S.C. 1002

children.label: D
children.text: the term “low or no emission vehicle component” means an item that is separately installed in and removable from a low or no emission vehicle.

children.heading: Assessing low or no emission vehicle components.—
children.label: 2 Assessing low or no emission vehicle components.—
i
children.text: to conduct testing, evaluation, and analysis of low or no emission vehicle components intended for use in low or no emission vehicles; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to conduct directed technology research.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into a contract or cooperative agreement with, or make a grant to, at least 1 institution of higher education to operate and maintain a facility to conduct testing, evaluation, and analysis of low or no emission vehicle components, and new and emerging technology components, intended for use in low or no emission vehicles.

children.label: I
children.text: capacity to carry out transportation-related advanced component and vehicle evaluation;

children.label: II
children.text: laboratories capable of testing and evaluation; and

children.label: III
children.text: direct access to or a partnership with a testing facility capable of emulating real-world circumstances in order to test low or no emission vehicle components installed on the intended vehicle.

children.heading: .—Fees
children.label: C .—Fees
children.text: A covered institution of higher education shall establish and collect fees, which shall be approved by the Secretary, for the assessment of low or no emission vehicle components at the applicable facility selected under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Availability of amounts to pay for assessment
children.label: D .—Availability of amounts to pay for assessment
i
children.text: the Secretary shall pay 50 percent of the cost of assessing a low or no emission vehicle component at the applicable facility selected under subparagraph (A) from amounts made available to carry out this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the remaining 50 percent of such cost shall be paid from amounts recovered through the fees established and collected pursuant to subparagraph (C).

children.heading: .—Voluntary testing
children.label: E .—Voluntary testing
children.text: A manufacturer of a low or no emission vehicle component is not required to assess the low or no emission vehicle component at a facility selected under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Compliance with section 5318
children.label: F .—Compliance with section 5318
children.text: Notwithstanding whether a low or no emission vehicle component is assessed at a facility selected under subparagraph (A), each new bus model shall comply with the requirements under section 5318.

children.heading: .—Separate facility
children.label: G .—Separate facility
children.text: A facility selected under subparagraph (A) shall be separate and distinct from the facility operated and maintained under section 5318.

children.label: i
children.text: acquisition of equipment and capital projects related to testing low or no emission vehicle components; or

children.label: ii
children.text: research related to advanced vehicle technologies that provides advancements to the entire public transportation industry.

children.heading: .—Cost share
children.label: I .—Cost share
children.text: The cost share for activities described in subparagraph (H) shall be subject to the terms in subsection (g).

children.heading: .—Low or no emission vehicle component performance reports
children.label: 3 .—Low or no emission vehicle component performance reports
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of the Federal Public Transportation Act of 2015, and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall issue a report on low or no emission vehicle component assessments conducted at each facility selected under paragraph (2)(A), which shall include information related to the maintainability, reliability, performance, structural integrity, efficiency, and noise of those low or no emission vehicle components, as applicable.

children.heading: .—Public availability of assessments
children.label: 4 .—Public availability of assessments
children.text: Each assessment conducted at a facility selected under paragraph (2)(A) shall be made publicly available, including to affected industries.

children.label: A
children.text: a low or no emission vehicle component to be tested at a facility selected under paragraph (2)(A); or

children.label: B
children.text: the development or disclosure of a privately funded component assessment.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The amounts made available under section 5338(a)(2)(G)(ii) are available for a public transportation cooperative research program.

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: A .—Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an independent governing board for the program under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Recommendations
children.label: B .—Recommendations
children.text: The board shall recommend public transportation research, development, and technology transfer activities the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Federal assistance
children.label: 3 .—Federal assistance
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to, and enter into cooperative agreements with, the National Academy of Sciences to carry out activities under this subsection that the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Government share of costs
children.label: 4 .—Government share of costs
children.text: If there would be a clear and direct financial benefit to an entity under a grant or contract financed under this subsection, the Secretary shall establish a Government share consistent with that benefit.

children.heading: .—Limitation on applicability
children.label: 5 .—Limitation on applicability
children.text: Subsections (f) and (g) shall not apply to activities carried out under this subsection.
